Verified with `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe --lib busy_retry_schedule_summary -- --nocapture`, which exercised:
- test_busy_retry_schedule_summary_captures_tail_latency_budget
- test_busy_retry_schedule_summary_bounds_wake_amplification
These tests lock in p50/p95/p99 spin-budget expectations plus bounded scheduler-yield amplification for the current engine retry policy. Keeping bd-db300.2.4.3 in progress until the bd-db300.2.4.2 policy-design bead is finished, because the validation narrative still depends on the final per-path policy statement.","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:34:27Z"},{"id":2118,"issue_id":"bd-db300.2.4.3","author":"IvoryBridge","text":"Claiming bd-db300.2.4.3 under the active bounded-handoff parent. Extending engine.rs with deterministic latency/fairness validation coverage for the new retry policy and keeping verification on rch only.","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:23:14.215Z"},{"id":2121,"issue_id":"bd-db300.2.4.3","author":"IvoryBridge","text":"Completed validation coverage for bounded handoff. Added deterministic schedule summaries plus p50/p95/p99 and wake-amplification assertions in crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs. Verification: rch cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe busy_ -- --nocapture passed; rch cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --tests passed; rch cargo fmt --check passed; targeted clippy still fails on the pre-existing record_value_type_metrics too_many_arguments lint in engine.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:31:46.284Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.2.4.4","title":"B4.4: run cross-subsystem contention validation for bounded handoff","description":"## Goal
run cross-subsystem contention validation for bounded handoff

## Checklist
1. Run the existing contention-storm and busy-semantics suites against the updated engine and BEGIN retry paths.
2. Confirm the bounded handoff changes do not regress MVCC, forced single-writer, or BUSY/BUSY_SNAPSHOT behavior at subsystem boundaries.
3. Capture replayable commands and outcome notes so the parent bead can distinguish validation evidence from still-missing hot-cell benchmark evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Run the existing contention-storm and busy-semantics suites against the updated engine and BEGIN retry paths.
2. Confirm the bounded handoff changes do not regress MVCC, forced single-writer, or BUSY/BUSY_SNAPSHOT behavior at subsystem boundaries.
3. Capture replayable commands and outcome notes so the parent bead can distinguish validation evidence from still-missing hot-cell benchmark evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Run the existing contention-storm and busy-semantics suites against the updated engine and BEGIN retry paths.
2. Confirm the bounded handoff changes do not regress MVCC, forced single-writer, or BUSY/BUSY_SNAPSHOT behavior at subsystem boundaries.
3. Capture replayable commands and outcome notes so the parent bead can distinguish validation evidence from still-missing hot-cell benchmark evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryBridge","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:47:05.462790679Z","created_by":"IvoryBridge","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:49:09.500793839Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["busy","concurrency","db300-program","db300-track-b","quality-gate","track-b","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.2.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:47:05.462790679Z","created_by":"IvoryBridge","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2128,"issue_id":"bd-db300.2.4.4","author":"IvoryBridge","text":"Starting cross-subsystem validation for the bounded-handoff changes. Planned verification surfaces:
1. `rch exec -- bash scripts/verify_bd_3plop_4_lock_contention_storms.sh` for lock-contention storm coverage across the harness stack.
2. `rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --test bd_2yqp6_6_2_busy_retry_error_semantics_matrix -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` for deterministic BUSY/BUSY_SNAPSHOT semantics at the engine/harness boundary.
Goal: prove the engine and BEGIN retry-path changes did not regress subsystem-level contention behavior, while explicitly separating this from the still-missing hot-cell benchmark evidence required by the parent bead.","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:47:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.3","title":"Track C: pager/WAL commit-path rewrite for batched publish","description":"## Goal
This track rewrites the pager/WAL commit path around batching and a much smaller serialized publish window. Static inspection showed that commits still hold an exclusive database lock while performing per-page frame allocation, copying, checksum work, and appends. That is the opposite of what a many-core writer path wants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The commit path has an explicit batch-append strategy with tests.
- The exclusive publish scope is shorter and easier to reason about.
- Allocation/copy/sort work on the commit path is measurably lower.","design":"- Serialize a whole transaction's WAL frames in one batch instead of page-by-page append loops.
- Move as much checksum/serialization/allocation work as possible out of the exclusive publish region.
- Remove page-1 and freelist/header work that needlessly turns every commit into a hot-page event.
- Keep durability and checksum semantics provably equivalent while changing shape and placement of work.","acceptance_criteria":"- The commit path has an explicit batch-append strategy with tests.
- The exclusive publish scope is shorter and easier to reason about.
- Allocation/copy/sort work on the commit path is measurably lower.","notes":"The strongest candidate found so far is batching a transaction's frames into one contiguous WAL write. There is already a helper in `fsqlite-wal` that suggests the project was heading in this direction, and there are checksum-equivalence tests that can anchor the refactor.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:46:31.460309535Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:30.130833816Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["copy-reduction","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-c","durability","group-commit","hot-path","pager","perf","track-c","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:57:10.623117491Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:57:46.671649131Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:58:22.610066534Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:58:56.796996143Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:59:31.885191082Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2106,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3","author":"codex","text":"Evidence snapshot motivating Track C:
- Static inspection found the WAL commit path holding an exclusive DB lock across a per-page append loop.
- Each page append currently allocates/copies frame bytes, computes checksums, and writes individually.
- Page-1/freelist/header work appears to create unnecessary hot-page behavior on every commit.
- The highest-EV immediate structural optimization is transaction-wide batch append with checksum-equivalence tests and a much smaller publish window.","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:47:55Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.3.1","title":"C1: implement transaction-wide batch WAL append with checksum-equivalence tests","description":"## Goal
Implement transaction-wide batch WAL append so a commit can serialize its frames once and write them as a contiguous batch instead of iterating page-by-page under a publish window. This is the highest-confidence structural win currently identified in the pager/WAL stack.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The commit path can append a whole transaction's frames in batch.
- Checksum-chain equivalence is covered by tests against the previous per-frame behavior.
- End-to-end benchmarks show whether the batch path materially reduces commit cost.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"- The commit path can append a whole transaction's frames in batch.
- Checksum-chain equivalence is covered by tests against the previous per-frame behavior.
- End-to-end benchmarks show whether the batch path materially reduces commit cost.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"This bead should leverage the existing clues in `fsqlite-wal`, including the group-commit helper and checksum-equivalence tests, rather than inventing a fresh direction from scratch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:55:46.287306080Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:30.361199355Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-c","durability","group-commit","hot-path","pager","perf","track-c","wal"],"comments":[{"id":2135,"issue_id":"bd-db300.3.1","author":"CobaltThicket","text":"Claimed bd-db300.3.1 and started evidence recovery. Fixed two compile blockers in crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_db300_3_1_batch_wal_append.rs by importing MvccPager and TransactionHandle so the parent verification surface can build. Child benchmark evidence for bd-db300.3.1.4 is now generated under artifacts/perf/bd-db300.3.1.4 (summary/report/benchmark/raw output); current median results show batch wins for 2-page and 32-page cases but loses for the 8-page hot cell, so the artifact bundle records mixed rather than all-green perf evidence. Parent gate script scripts/verify_bd_db300_3_1_batch_wal_append.sh is still running through its staged rch-offloaded checks. MCP Agent Mail remains unavailable from this workspace due repeated ensure_project/health timeouts, so Beads JSONL is the current coordination fallback.","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:23:42Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-db300.3.3","title":"C3: remove unnecessary page-1, freelist, and header work from every commit","description":"## Goal
Eliminate page-1, freelist, and header work that is being forced into every commit even when it is not semantically necessary. This bead exists because extra page-1 churn both burns cycles and increases the chance that unrelated writers collide on an artificial hot page.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The conditions under which page-1/freelist/header work is required are explicit.
- Unnecessary commit-time page-1 churn is removed or deferred.
- Contention and/or commit cost on relevant workloads improves measurably.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"- The conditions under which page-1/freelist/header work is required are explicit.
- Unnecessary commit-time page-1 churn is removed or deferred.
- Contention and/or commit cost on relevant workloads improves measurably.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"The important distinction here is between correctness-required metadata work and habitual work that merely 'always happened'. +{"id":"bd-db300.3.3","title":"C3: remove unnecessary page-1, freelist, and header work from every commit","description":"## Goal
Eliminate page-1, freelist, and header work that is being forced into every commit even when it is not semantically necessary. This bead exists because extra page-1 churn both burns cycles and increases the chance that unrelated writers collide on an artificial hot page.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The conditions under which page-1/freelist/header work is required are explicit.
- Unnecessary commit-time page-1 churn is removed or deferred.
- Contention and/or commit cost on relevant workloads improves measurably.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"- The conditions under which page-1/freelist/header work is required are explicit.
- Unnecessary commit-time page-1 churn is removed or deferred.
- Contention and/or commit cost on relevant workloads improves measurably.
- Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
- Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"The important distinction here is between correctness-required metadata work and habitual work that merely 'always happened'.
